role_title: Sr. Manager, Digital Ordering - Strategy & Operations
secondary_archetype: Technical Program Manager (product roadmap steering + GTM ops)

level_signals: Manage a team of strategy & operations colleagues
Base range $170K-$250K consistent with Senior Manager at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You are a strong strategic thinker and have great intuitive product sense
you are a builder at heart, and have already adopted AI tools beyond ChatGPT to meaningfully improve how you work
You have experience in a complex or high-growth environment (high-growth SaaS experience is nice-to-have, but not required)
You have taken something from 0>1 (former founders welcome) and operate comfortably in ambiguous environments
You imagine, develop, and execute on workstreams from start to completion with little oversight
You oscillate easily from strategic considerations to tactical execution
You collaborate effectively with cross-functional team members across the organization
You represents the DoorDash brand with humility and a customer-first mindset
Manage a team of strategy & operations colleagues
Analyze complex data sets paired with partner team feedback to provide insights and recommendations to our leadership

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: high-growth SaaS experience is nice-to-have, but not required

scope.surface: DoorDash Digital Ordering — B2B SaaS suite (online ordering, branded mobile apps, loyalty solutions) for merchants across Americas
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS (first-party digital ordering for restaurants, grocers, retailers)
core_responsibilities: Lead high visibility, cross-functional projects addressing Digital Ordering's most unique and challenging questions
Build strategies and drive execution across go-to-market priorities including inbound lead generation, selection intelligence, rep productivity
Steer the Digital Ordering product roadmap informed by quantitative analysis and qualitative merchant feedback
Develop and execute strategies related to how Digital Ordering fits into the broader industry landscape
Execute product testing and roll-outs on the ground with merchant partners
Collaborate with stakeholders across DoorDash to align company-wide resources to refine platform capabilities
Analyze complex data sets and provide insights and recommendations to leadership
Manage a team of strategy & operations colleagues

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($170K-$250K base) and competitive for Senior Manager level
Equity grants offered in addition to base
Role sits in one of DoorDash's fastest-growing segments — meaningful impact opportunity
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Posting deadline stated (May 1, 2026) — signals active, time-bound search
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous — JD references NYC-specific Covey AEDT notices and 'Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' but does not clearly state whether role is remote, hybrid, or onsite
High breadth of responsibilities (product roadmap steering AND GTM strategy AND execution AND people management) could indicate role scope is poorly defined or understaffed
Filling deadline of May 1, 2026 has likely already passed at time of fetch (fetched 2026-05-10)

ambiguities: Location type is unclear: the JD body does not explicitly state remote, hybrid, or onsite. The NYC Covey AEDT notice references 'Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC,' suggesting remote eligibility but not confirming it. Treated as 'unclear' with remote_us_eligible: true as a soft inference.

Role description covers both product roadmap ownership and GTM strategy/operations — JD says 'either leading new products… or overseeing our GTM strategy' suggesting this may be one of two distinct roles being filled under the same posting, which creates ambiguity about actual scope.
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD; left as 'unclear'.

The requirement 'You are a builder at heart, and have already adopted AI tools beyond ChatGPT' is phrased as a trait under 'We're excited about you because…' — treated as hard given unconditional phrasing in a requirements section, but could be interpreted as aspirational.
